exam.title: MSc Admission Test Preparation
instructions: Answer all fifty questions. Each question carries 3 marks.
There is no negative marking. The qualifying mark is 60 (40%).

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.question: The trace of a square matrix is defined as:

options.b: the sum of its main-diagonal elements

questions.explanation: The trace is the sum of the diagonal entries (which also equals the sum of the eigenvalues).

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.question: What is the derivative of $e^x$ with respect to x?
questions.explanation: The exponential function eˣ is its own derivative.

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.question: Solve the equation $|x - 3| = 5$.
questions.explanation: x − 3 = 5 gives x = 8, and x − 3 = −5 gives x = −2.

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.question: How many diagonals does a hexagon (6-sided polygon) have?
questions.explanation: Diagonals = n(n−3)/2 = 6·3/2 = 9.

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.question: What is the determinant of the 3×3 identity matrix?
questions.explanation: The identity matrix is upper triangular with 1's on the diagonal, so its determinant is 1.

questions.subject: Statistics
questions.question: The standard normal distribution has which mean and standard deviation?
questions.explanation: The standard normal distribution N(0, 1) is defined to have mean 0 and standard deviation 1.

questions.subject: Statistics
questions.question: A single card is drawn from a standard 52-card deck. What is the probability that it is an ace?
questions.explanation: There are 4 aces out of 52 cards, so the probability is 4/52 = 1/13.

questions.subject: Statistics
questions.question: The range of a data set is calculated as:

options.b: the maximum minus the minimum value

questions.explanation: Range is a simple dispersion measure: largest value minus smallest value.

questions.subject: Theory of Computation (TOC)
questions.question: A pushdown automaton (PDA) is a finite automaton augmented with:
questions.explanation: A PDA adds a stack to a finite automaton, giving it the power to recognize context-free languages.

questions.subject: Theory of Computation (TOC)
questions.question: Which of the following problems is undecidable?
questions.explanation: Deciding whether two context-free grammars generate the same language is undecidable; the others are decidable.

questions.subject: Theory of Computation (TOC)
questions.question: The Kleene star of the empty set, ∅*, equals:

options.b: {ε} (the set containing only the empty string)

questions.explanation: Kleene star always includes the empty string (zero concatenations), so ∅* = {ε}.

questions.subject: Theory of Computation (TOC)
questions.question: A context-free grammar is ambiguous if:

options.b: some string has two or more distinct parse trees

questions.explanation: Ambiguity means at least one string in the language has more than one parse tree (or leftmost derivation).

questions.subject: Programming
questions.question: What is the output of this C code?
questions.explanation: Left-shifting by 1 multiplies by 2, so 2 << 1 = 4.

questions.subject: Programming
questions.question: What does the following C code print?
questions.explanation: p points to x; assigning *p = 20 writes through the pointer, changing x to 20.

questions.subject: Programming
questions.question: Which Java collection type does NOT allow duplicate elements?
questions.explanation: A Set models a mathematical set and rejects duplicate elements; List implementations allow duplicates.

questions.subject: Programming
questions.question: In C++, a class destructor is automatically called when:

options.b: the object goes out of scope or is deleted
options.d: never; it must be called manually

questions.explanation: The destructor runs when an object's lifetime ends — it leaves scope or is explicitly deleted.

questions.subject: Programming
questions.question: What is the final value of sum printed by this C code?
questions.explanation: The inner loop runs 2 times for each of the 3 outer iterations, so sum is incremented 3 × 2 = 6 times.

questions.subject: Data Structures & Algorithms (DSA)
questions.question: Which data structure does a runtime use to manage function calls and recursion?
questions.explanation: Function activation records are pushed/popped on the call stack, matching the LIFO nature of calls and returns.

questions.subject: Data Structures & Algorithms (DSA)
questions.question: What is the worst-case search time in a hash table when every key hashes to the same bucket?
questions.explanation: If all keys collide into one chain, a search degenerates to scanning a list of n items, i.e. O(n).

questions.subject: Data Structures & Algorithms (DSA)
questions.question: In a circular linked list:

options.b: the last node's pointer references the first node
options.d: each node has two data fields

questions.explanation: A circular linked list connects the last node back to the first, forming a loop with no NULL terminator.

questions.subject: Data Structures & Algorithms (DSA)
questions.question: Topological sorting can be performed only on:
questions.explanation: A topological order exists only if the directed graph has no cycles, i.e. it is a DAG.

questions.subject: Data Structures & Algorithms (DSA)
questions.question: What is the average-case time complexity of quicksort?
questions.explanation: With balanced partitions on average, quicksort runs in O(n log n); its worst case is O(n²).

questions.subject: Data Structures & Algorithms (DSA)
questions.question: Which collision-resolution technique stores a colliding key in the next available slot of the table itself?
questions.explanation: Open addressing probes for another empty slot within the table rather than using external chains.

questions.subject: Computer Architecture
questions.question: What is the decimal value of the hexadecimal number FF?
questions.explanation: FF₁₆ = 15×16 + 15 = 255 (the largest value in one byte).

questions.subject: Computer Architecture
questions.question: In a pipelined processor, a branch instruction most directly causes which kind of hazard?
questions.explanation: A branch changes the instruction flow, so the pipeline may have fetched wrong-path instructions — a control (branch) hazard.

questions.subject: Computer Architecture
questions.question: Which type of memory is non-volatile (retains data without power)?
questions.explanation: ROM keeps its contents when powered off; RAM, cache, and registers are volatile.

questions.subject: Computer Architecture
questions.question: How many bits are there in one byte?
questions.explanation: By standard definition a byte consists of 8 bits.

questions.subject: Operating Systems (OS)
questions.question: A Process Control Block (PCB) stores:

options.b: process state, program counter, and register contents

questions.explanation: The PCB holds all per-process metadata — state, PC, registers, scheduling info — needed to manage and resume it.

questions.subject: Operating Systems (OS)
questions.question: Mutual exclusion over a shared resource is commonly implemented using:
questions.explanation: Semaphores (or mutexes) provide the synchronization primitive used to guarantee mutual exclusion.

questions.subject: Operating Systems (OS)
questions.question: In the producer-consumer problem, access to the shared bounded buffer requires:
questions.explanation: Producers and consumers share the buffer, so semaphores/mutexes are needed to avoid races and over/underflow.

questions.subject: Operating Systems (OS)
questions.question: Which scheduling algorithm is best suited to giving good response time in interactive, time-sharing systems?
questions.explanation: Round Robin's small time quantum gives every process frequent CPU turns, keeping interactive response times low.

questions.subject: Operating Systems (OS)
questions.question: Spooling is most commonly used to manage:

options.b: slow I/O devices such as printers

questions.explanation: Spooling queues jobs (e.g. print jobs) on disk so a slow device can process them while the CPU continues other work.

questions.subject: Computer Networks
questions.question: Which network topology connects every device to a single central hub or switch?
questions.explanation: In a star topology each node has a dedicated link to a central device, which is also a single point of failure.

questions.subject: Computer Networks
questions.question: The protocol data unit at the network (IP) layer is called a:
questions.explanation: The network layer handles packets; segments belong to the transport layer and frames to the data link layer.

questions.subject: Computer Networks
questions.question: Which transport-layer protocol is connection-oriented and guarantees reliable, ordered delivery?
questions.explanation: TCP establishes a connection and provides reliable, in-order delivery through acknowledgements and retransmission.

questions.subject: Computer Networks
questions.question: How many bits are in a standard MAC (hardware) address?
questions.explanation: A MAC address is 48 bits, usually written as six pairs of hexadecimal digits.

questions.subject: Database Management Systems (DBMS)
questions.question: Which SQL statement is used to retrieve data from a table?
questions.explanation: SELECT is the SQL query statement used to read rows from one or more tables.

questions.subject: Database Management Systems (DBMS)
questions.question: A composite key is:

options.a: a foreign key referencing another table
options.b: a key formed from two or more attributes together
options.d: a uniqueness constraint on a single column

questions.explanation: A composite (compound) key uses a combination of two or more columns to uniquely identify a row.

questions.subject: Database Management Systems (DBMS)
questions.question: Which transaction isolation level prevents dirty reads but still permits non-repeatable reads?
questions.explanation: Read Committed forbids reading uncommitted data (no dirty reads) but a row may change between two reads in the same transaction.

questions.subject: Database Management Systems (DBMS)
questions.question: Which SQL join produces the Cartesian product of two tables?
questions.explanation: A CROSS JOIN pairs every row of the first table with every row of the second, yielding the Cartesian product.

questions.subject: Distributed Systems
questions.question: A Distributed Hash Table (DHT) provides:

options.a: centralized data storage on one server
options.b: a decentralized lookup that maps keys to the nodes responsible for them

questions.explanation: A DHT spreads key-value pairs across nodes and lets any node locate the responsible node without a central directory.

questions.subject: Distributed Systems
questions.question: The Byzantine Generals problem is about reaching agreement when some participants may:

options.b: behave arbitrarily or maliciously (send conflicting information)

questions.explanation: Byzantine fault tolerance addresses consensus despite components that can fail arbitrarily, including lying.

questions.subject: Distributed Systems
questions.question: Horizontal scaling (scaling out) refers to:

options.a: upgrading a single server's CPU and RAM
options.b: adding more machines/nodes to share the load

questions.explanation: Horizontal scaling adds more nodes to distribute work; vertical scaling upgrades a single machine.

questions.subject: Artificial Intelligence (AI)
questions.question: Alpha-beta pruning improves which algorithm by skipping branches that cannot affect the result?
questions.explanation: Alpha-beta pruning prunes subtrees in minimax game-tree search, reducing the number of nodes evaluated.

questions.subject: Artificial Intelligence (AI)
questions.question: In informed (heuristic) search, the heuristic function h(n) estimates:

options.a: the cost already spent reaching node n
options.b: the estimated cost from node n to the goal
options.d: the branching factor of the tree

questions.explanation: The heuristic h(n) approximates the remaining cost from n to a goal, guiding the search toward promising nodes.

questions.subject: Artificial Intelligence (AI)
questions.question: The Naive Bayes classifier makes which simplifying assumption about features?

options.b: features are conditionally independent given the class
options.d: features are irrelevant to the class

questions.explanation: Naive Bayes assumes class-conditional independence of features, which simplifies probability estimation.

questions.subject: Artificial Intelligence (AI)
questions.question: Which of the following is an unsupervised learning algorithm?
questions.explanation: K-means groups unlabelled data into clusters without target labels, making it unsupervised; the others are supervised.

questions.subject: Analytical Ability
questions.question: A person walks 3 km due north, then 4 km due east. How far are they from the starting point?
questions.explanation: The displacement is the hypotenuse of a 3-4 right triangle: √(3² + 4²) = √25 = 5 km.

questions.subject: Analytical Ability
questions.question: Find the next term in the series: AZ, BY, CX, ?
questions.explanation: The first letter advances A→B→C→D while the second moves back Z→Y→X→W, giving DW.

questions.subject: Analytical Ability
questions.question: If 5 machines take 5 minutes to make 5 widgets, how long will 100 machines take to make 100 widgets?
questions.explanation: Each machine makes one widget in 5 minutes, so 100 machines make 100 widgets in the same 5 minutes.
